title: Jewish Precedents for Jesus Becoming God
author: BDEhrman
description: Surveys Jewish and biblical precedents for figures other than the one God being called divine, setting up an explanation for early claims about Jesus' divine status.

title: How Can Monotheists Think a Human Could Become God?
author: BDEhrman
description: Uses early Jewish texts about righteous humans becoming angels to explain how Jewish monotheists could imagine a human being exalted to divine status.

title: August 2026 Gold Q&A Announcement
author: jennao
description: Announces the August 2026 Gold and Platinum member Q&A session and provides details for submitting questions and joining the live Zoom event.

title: Personal Faith, Textual Problems, and the Miracles of Jesus: More Readers’ Questions
author: BDEhrman
description: Answers reader questions about faith and biblical criticism, a difficult textual variant in 2 Peter, and how miracle stories about Jesus may have developed.

title: How the Year 1517 Revolutionized Biblical Studies
author: BDEhrman
description: Traces how Luther's 95 Theses and appeal to scriptural authority helped create conditions for historical-critical study of canon, authorship, and biblical discrepancies.

title: Two Years that Transformed the World of Biblical Studies.
author: BDEhrman
description: Explains how Erasmus's first published Greek New Testament helped launch modern biblical scholarship and raised the textual questions that led to New Testament textual criticism.

title: The Martyrdom of Polycarp (Another Book of the Apostolic Fathers): In a Nutshell
author: BDEhrman
description: Introduces The Martyrdom of Polycarp as the earliest surviving Christian account of a martyr's trial and death outside the New Testament.

title: 2 Clement (one of the “Apostolic Fathers”) in a Nutshell
author: BDEhrman
description: Introduces 2 Clement as the earliest surviving Christian homily outside the New Testament and explains its significance.

title: Christian Justifications for Lying
author: BDEhrman
description: Examines whether early Christian forgers could justify deception as a noble lie, contrasting that view with Augustine's rejection of all lying.

title: Ancient Christians Who Wanted to Lie
author: BDEhrman
description: Explores why ancient Christian authors might have considered lying acceptable when promoting what they regarded as religious truth.

title: Evangelicals Who Lie to Promote the(ir) Truth….
author: BDEhrman
description: Uses the First-Century Mark controversy to argue that some evangelical advocates defend their views by repeating or promoting false claims.

title: Free Course on … Hell!
author: BDEhrman
description: Announces a free online course on Jesus, the Bible, and the development of Christian ideas about hell and eternal punishment.

title: Exonerating Pilate to Implicate the Jews
author: BDEhrman
description: Traces how Christian accounts increasingly exonerated Pilate while shifting blame for Jesus' death onto Jews and Jewish leaders.

title: Jesus the Forager
author: BDEhrman
description: Tests whether Gospel passages support the idea that Jesus' group sometimes survived by foraging during the ministry.

title: Foraging in the World of Jesus
author: BDEhrman
description: Explains why foraging may fit the economic world of Jesus, Galilee, and the largely agrarian Roman Empire.

title: Pro-Roman Jews in First-Century Palestine? Guest Post by Christopher Stanley
author: jennao
description: Introduces Christopher Stanley's argument that some Jews in first-century Palestine may have opposed revolt and sympathized with Rome.

title: Did Jesus Have Wealthy Donors?
author: BDEhrman
description: Evaluates whether wealthy donors supported Jesus' group, especially in light of Luke's reference to women who provided for the ministry.

title: How Did Jesus and His Disciples Get Enough to Eat?
author: BDEhrman
description: Raises the historical question of how Jesus and the Twelve ate while living as itinerant preachers without ordinary income.

title: The Execution of Pontius Pilate for Killing Jesus (!)
author: BDEhrman
description: Presents and contextualizes the apocryphal Handing Over of Pilate, where Pilate is punished by Tiberius but repents and turns to Christ.

title: July 2026 Gold Q&A Announcement
author: jennao
description: Announces the July 2026 Gold and Platinum member Q&A session and provides the meeting details for participants.

title: A Blog Dinner In Central London July 15.  Wanna Come?
author: BDEhrman
description: Invites blog members to a July 15 dinner in central London and explains the logistics for attending.

title: Did Pontius Pilate Write a Letter Explaining Why He Crucified Jesus?
author: BDEhrman
description: Introduces and translates the apocryphal Report of Pilate, a letter portraying Jesus' trial, death, and resurrection from Pilate's perspective.

title: Jesus and the Gospel of John: Some Readers’ Good Questions
author: BDEhrman
description: Answers reader questions about the Gospel of John, including its presentation of Jesus, authorship, and historical reliability.

title: How Do You Publish a Book for a General Audience?
author: BDEhrman
description: Explains the practical difficulties scholars face when trying to publish trade books for general readers.

title: The Realities of Publishing a Scholarly Book
author: BDEhrman
description: Explains how scholarly publishing depends on academic credentials, specialized training, and the expectations of academic publishers.

title: Memory of My Past: My First Girlfriend and Jesus
author: BDEhrman
description: Recounts a high-school memory of becoming born again and trying to convert a Jewish former girlfriend.

title: Did Paul Have an Exalted View of Himself?
author: BDEhrman
description: Argues that Paul understood himself as specially appointed to bring Gentiles into God's people without adopting Jewish law.

title: The Most Likely Capitalist Parable of Jesus?
author: BDEhrman
description: Interprets the Parable of the Talents and Pounds as apocalyptic teaching rather than an endorsement of capitalist investing.

title: The Parable of the Sower as Advice for Capitalists
author: BDEhrman
description: Rejects capitalist readings of the Parable of the Sower by reading it in Mark's context as a warning against wealth.

title: The Capitalist Parables of Jesus
author: BDEhrman
description: Critiques attempts to make Jesus into a capitalist, socialist, or Marxist by stressing how interpreters often remake Jesus in their own image.

title: Why Not Believe in a God Who is *Not* Active in the World?
author: BDEhrman
description: Explains why belief in a noninterventionist God does not resolve the problem of suffering that led to atheism.

title: How To Figure Out If a Miracle Happened…  Questions from Readers
author: BDEhrman
description: Explains why historians can discuss miracle claims but cannot establish supernatural intervention by ordinary historical methods.

title: More Criticisms of the Criticisms of the Gospel of John (by John! Spong)
author: BDEhrman
description: Continues engaging John Shelby Spong's interpretation of the Gospel of John, especially Spong's claims about John's Christology and symbolism.

title: Controversies About the Gospel of John: The Views of John Spong
author: BDEhrman
description: Engages John Shelby Spong's skeptical reading of John and compares Spong's public presentation of scholarship with critical views of the Gospel's historicity.

title: Radical Skeptic (about the Bible) and Episcopal Bishop: John Spong
author: BDEhrman
description: Remembers John Shelby Spong as an Episcopal bishop who brought critical biblical scholarship to broad Christian audiences.

title: Questions on Proving the Resurrection and Sundry Other Things
author: BDEhrman
description: Answers reader questions about proving the resurrection, eyewitness claims, composite Pauline letters, miracles, and John the Baptist.

title: These Are Weird Parables.  Do They Make Sense?
author: BDEhrman
description: Introduces two puzzling Lukan parables about wealth, the Rich Fool and the Dishonest Steward, and asks how they fit together.

title: Did the Doctrine of Predestination Lead to Capitalism?
author: BDEhrman
description: Explains Max Weber's argument that Protestant predestination, especially in Calvinist thought, helped shape the modern capitalist drive for disciplined profit.

title: Predestination!  What do you think?
author: BDEhrman
description: Surveys biblical passages and theological problems connected with predestination, grace, divine sovereignty, free will, Augustine, Calvin, and double predestination.

title: Doesn’t Goodness Point to the Existence of God?  And Gospel Perplexities.  Good Readers’ Questions
author: BDEhrman
description: Responds to reader questions about goodness and suffering, Luke's presentation of Jesus' divine sonship, and how anonymous gospels circulated.

title: Did the Glories of Martyrdom Lead to Christian Conversions?
author: BDEhrman
description: Examines whether stories of Christian martyrs' endurance under torture helped persuade outsiders to convert.

title: June 2026 Gold Q&A Announcement
author: jennao
description: Announces the June 2026 Gold and Platinum member Q&A, including the format, question deadline, live participation instructions, and planned focus on Paul's letters.

title: The Fear of Hell as an Incentive to Convert
author: BDEhrman
description: Explains how Christian preaching about hell could make conversion seem urgent, especially when paired with stories of divine power and miracles.

title: Was Augustine Telling the Truth About Miracles He’d Seen?
author: BDEhrman
description: Examines Augustine's reports of miracles as evidence for how miracle claims functioned in Christian arguments for conversion.

title: Biographical Accounts of Early Christian Miracles (Based on Eyewitnesses!)
author: BDEhrman
description: Discusses later Christian biographies of Gregory the Wonderworker and Martin of Tours as miracle-centered stories of conversion and religious power.

title: And the Miracles Just Keep on Comin’
author: BDEhrman
description: Surveys legendary miracle stories outside the New Testament, including traditions about Edessa, Thaddaeus, John, and Peter.

title: How Could Christian Miracles Convert the Empire if Miracles Don’t Happen?
author: BDEhrman
description: Explains how miracle stories could promote conversion even if the miracles themselves did not happen.

title: Why Christian Miracles Converted the Empire
author: BDEhrman
description: Argues that early Christian conversion accounts most often explain Christian success by appealing to miracles.

title: Superior Health Care as an Explanation for the Spread of Christianity?
author: BDEhrman
description: Evaluates the modern claim that Christianity spread because Christian communities provided better health care during epidemics.

title: A Modern “Common Sense” About What Made Christianity Attractive to Converts
author: BDEhrman
description: Examines the claim that Christianity attracted converts because Christian communities offered unusually strong social support.

title: What an Ancient Enemy of Christianity Said About Why It Was Successful
author: BDEhrman
description: Uses Celsus's critique of Christianity to show how one ancient pagan opponent understood Christian success.

title: How Did Christianity Succeed?  An Older View That Many People Still Have
author: BDEhrman
description: Critiques an older scholarly explanation for Christianity's success and contrasts it with conversion, exclusivity, and mission.

title: Do You Know The Golden Ass?   (Is a Mystery Religion like Christianity?)
author: BDEhrman
description: Introduces Apuleius's The Golden Ass as evidence for ancient mystery religion and Greco-Roman religious experience.

title: Christianity:  A Weirdly Exclusivist Religion
author: BDEhrman
description: Explains how Christian exclusivity made the movement unusual in the Roman world and helped reshape religious allegiance.

title: Some Important Readers’ Questions on Some Gospel Head-Scratchers
author: BDEhrman
description: Responds to reader questions about Luke's Christology, Gospel tensions, and how New Testament writers handle Jesus' identity.

title: How Early Christians Made Converts.  (Tent revivals?)
author: BDEhrman
description: Examines how early Christians made converts without modern-style rallies, mass campaigns, or organized door-to-door evangelism.

title: Converting the World:  Why Has Christianity Always Been “Missionary”?
author: BDEhrman
description: Explores why Christianity became a missionary religion and why that feature mattered for its spread in the Roman world.

title: Blog Dinner in Waynesville NC, May 19.  Wanna Come?
author: BDEhrman
description: Announces a blog dinner in Waynesville, North Carolina, with attendance details for interested members.

title: Jesus and Capitalism:  My Next Book (A Big Change)
author: BDEhrman
description: Announces a planned book on Jesus, capitalism, socialism, and related economic questions from a New Testament perspective.

title: Want To Be Involved in More In-Depth Discussion of Key Issues?  A Blog Opportunity
author: BDEhrman
description: Introduces the Blog Stewards group as a way for members to discuss New Testament and early Christianity topics in more depth.

title: A Common But Lousy Argument That We KNOW What the NT Originally Said: Anniversary Post #14
author: BDEhrman
description: Critiques the argument that abundant New Testament manuscripts prove secure access to the original text.

title: Sailing Cruise to Caribbean Islands in January.  Want to Come with Me?
author: BDEhrman
description: Announces a January 2027 Caribbean sailing cruise and describes the trip format, ship, and lecture component.

title: The Morality of War
author: BDEhrman
description: Reflects on the morality of war while explaining why the blog normally avoids direct political commentary.

title: May 2026 Gold Q&A Announcement: We’re Trying Something New!
author: jennao
description: Announces a May 2026 Gold and Platinum member Q&A with a revised format that includes live participant questions.

title: Nile Cruise: Cancellation
author: BDEhrman
description: Announces the cancellation or postponement of the planned Nile cruise because of conditions in the Middle East.

title: Was Jesus the Incarnation of an Angel?  Anniversary Post #13
author: BDEhrman
description: Explains the argument that Paul understood Christ's pre-existent state in angelic terms.

title: An Amazing Fragment of a Lost Gospel: Anniversary Post #12
author: BDEhrman
description: Discusses a fragmentary lost gospel, possibly related to the Gospel of Peter, featuring a conversation between Jesus and Peter.

title: Active Pastors Who Have Lost Their Faith: Anniversary Post #11
author: BDEhrman
description: Discusses pastors who remain in ministry after losing belief and why leaving the pulpit can be difficult.

title: The Seven Sleepers of Ephesus: Platinum Post by Douglas Wadeson, MD
author: jennao
description: Presents the legend of the Seven Sleepers of Ephesus, including its Christian setting and later appearance in the Qur'an.

title: A Letter Written by Jesus!?  Anniversary Post #10
author: BDEhrman
description: Discusses ancient traditions claiming that Jesus corresponded with King Abgar of Edessa.

title: Anniversary Post #9:  Misquoting Misquoting Jesus
author: BDEhrman
description: Explains how Misquoting Jesus has often been misunderstood, especially by critics concerned about its implications for faith.

title: May 2026 Platinum Webinar Announcement
author: jennao
description: Announces a Platinum member webinar on the Gospel of Thomas with date, time, access details, and replay information.

title: Different Words, VERY Different Theologies, and Understanding Which Words They Were. Readers’ Questions
author: BDEhrman
description: Responds to reader questions about resurrection language, textual wording, and theological meaning in New Testament interpretation.

title: Bad Bible Jokes.  (And do you have any?)
author: BDEhrman
description: Collects Bible jokes prompted by Genesis and other biblical allusions, while inviting readers to add more.

title: Anniversary Post #8:  When Is a Contradiction Not a Contradiction?
author: BDEhrman
description: Explains what counts as a contradiction in Gospel comparison and why definitions matter in debates about biblical discrepancies.

title: Does God Care What We Wear? A Platinum Post by Douglas Wadeson, MD
author: jennao
description: Examines religious clothing rules in Judaism, Christianity, and Islam, asking whether dress regulations have scriptural grounding.

title: Celebrating our 14th Anniversary!  Check Out the Unusual Q&A!
author: BDEhrman
description: Shares the recording of a fourteenth-anniversary Q&A built around playful historical hypotheticals.

title: Anniversary Post #7:  Doing a Graduate Degree in Early Christian Studies
author: BDEhrman
description: Explains why pursuing a PhD in New Testament or early Christianity is far more demanding than many prospective students expect.

title: Anniversary Post #6  Is Mark’s Seemingly Simple Gospel Unsophisticated?
author: BDEhrman
description: Argues that Mark's Gospel is more literarily sophisticated than its simple style may first suggest.

title: Anniversary Post #5: Why I Was Reluctant to Write The Triumph of Christianity
author: BDEhrman
description: Explains why writing The Triumph of Christianity seemed unusually difficult because of the scope of the historical question.

title: Anniversary Post #4: Why Gospels Matter Even Where They Are Not Historical
author: BDEhrman
description: Argues that the Gospels can matter even when particular stories are not historically accurate, because memory also shapes meaning.

title: Anniversary Post #3:  My Response to an Ill-Tempered Richard Carrier
author: BDEhrman
description: Responds to Richard Carrier's criticism of the claim that first-century pagan sources do not mention Pontius Pilate.

title: Anniversary Post #2: Why Were the Gospels Written Anonymously?
author: BDEhrman
description: Explains why the New Testament Gospels circulated anonymously and why that differs from some other ancient writings.

title: Anniversary Post #1:  Defending Misquoting Jesus
author: BDEhrman
description: Defends Misquoting Jesus against criticisms that the book exaggerated or distorted the problem of textual variation.

title: Celebrating The Blog’s 14th Anniversary!  Do You Have a Favorite Post?
author: BDEhrman
description: Marks the blog's fourteenth anniversary by inviting readers to nominate favorite posts for republication.

title: The Distinctively Jewish Roots of Jesus’ Ethics
author: BDEhrman
description: Shows how Jesus' ethical teachings stand within Jewish scripture and Jewish moral teaching rather than apart from them.

title: Understanding the Gospels, Jesus, and the Spread of Christianity: Great Readers’ Questions
author: BDEhrman
description: Responds to reader questions about Jewish proselytizing, Christian word-of-mouth conversion, and Jesus' Son of Man sayings.

title: Rethinking Faith Podcast Interview About Love Thy Stranger
author: jennao
description: Discusses a Rethinking Faith podcast interview about Love Thy Stranger and the book's argument about Jesus' ethical legacy.

title: Early Christian Reactions to “Heresies” in a Nutshell
author: BDEhrman
description: Summarizes early Christian reactions to heresy and the role those disputes played in defining orthodox belief.

title: The First Attempts to Wipe Out Christianity
author: BDEhrman
description: Explains why early persecutions were limited before the mid-third century and how later threats to Christianity became more serious.

title: When Emperors Became More Involved in Christian Persecutions
author: BDEhrman
description: Explains how imperial involvement made Christian persecution more organized and dangerous in the Roman world.

title: You’re Invited: The Blog Turns 14
author: BDEhrman
description: Invites readers to a fourteenth-anniversary blog event.

title: Early Persecutions of Christians, in a Nutshell
author: BDEhrman
description: Summarizes early persecutions of Christians before empire-wide suppression became a sustained threat.

title: The Rise of Christian Anti-Judaism, in a Nutshell
author: BDEhrman
description: Summarizes the rise of Christian anti-Judaism and the debate over whether followers of Jesus should keep Jewish law.

title: April 2026 Gold Q&A: Get Your Questions In!
author: jennao
description: Announces the April 2026 Gold Q&A and invites members to submit questions.

title: Early Christian Views of Judaism, In a Nutshell
author: BDEhrman
description: Surveys early Christian views of Judaism, especially whether following Jesus required keeping Jewish customs and laws.

title: Readers’ Questions on the Accuracy of the Gospels
author: BDEhrman
description: Responds to reader questions about Gospel accuracy, historical reliability, and how contradictions affect interpretation.

title: The Good Done By Christianity to Our World
author: BDEhrman
description: Considers Christianity's moral legacy by weighing harms done in Christian history against ethical ideals shaped by Jesus' teachings.

title: The Dark Side of Christianity: How I (Partially) End My New Book
author: BDEhrman
description: Previews the conclusion of Love Thy Stranger by discussing Christianity's darker legacy alongside its influence on Western moral conscience.

title: Advance Preview–How I Begin My New Book: Love Thy Stranger
author: BDEhrman
description: Introduces the opening of Love Thy Stranger and explains how the book frames Jesus' teachings about moral obligation to strangers.

title: The Letter to Diognetus: An Unknown Gem among the Apostolic Fathers (in a Nutshell)
author: BDEhrman
description: Introduces the Letter to Diognetus as an early Christian apology and a notable work among the Apostolic Fathers.

title: Why in Particular I’d Love to Get my Grubby Paws on Paul’s Lost Letters
author: BDEhrman
description: Explains why lost letters of Paul would be especially valuable for understanding Paul's thought and influence.

title: Paul’s Lost Letters
author: BDEhrman
description: Discusses the lost letters of Paul and why additional Pauline writings would matter for early Christian history.

title: While We’re Talking About the Reliability of Eyewitnesses…

description: Uses research on eyewitness memory to question simple appeals to eyewitness reliability.

title: Eyewitness Accounts of Miracles
author: BDEhrman
description: Examines eyewitness miracle reports in another religious tradition as a test case for claims about resurrection testimony.

title: Christ’s legalism, his divine and human nature, stories of his father Joseph — and other questions.
author: BDEhrman
description: Responds to reader questions about Jesus' relation to law, Christ's divine and human nature, and traditions about Joseph.

title: A Legal Case for Jesus’s Resurrection
author: BDEhrman
description: Uses a hypothetical murder case to test whether legal reasoning can establish Jesus' resurrection historically.

title: A New Proof of the Resurrection.  What Do YOU Think?
author: BDEhrman
description: Discusses a debate with Jonathan Sheffield over a proposed proof of Jesus' resurrection.

title: Does God Care What We Eat? – Platinum Post by Doug Wadeson
author: jennao
description: Presents a Platinum member post about biblical and religious food rules.

title: How Important Actually Was Paul (When Alive and After)?
author: BDEhrman
description: Examines Paul's importance during life, later Christian memory of Paul, and the reliability of traditions about Paul.

title: Does Papias Say Matthew and Mark Wrote OUR Matthew and Mark?
author: BDEhrman
description: Argues that Papias's comments about Matthew and Mark do not clearly refer to the canonical Gospels now known by those names.

title: Do the Synoptics Present an Early Character of the Jesus Movement?  –Platinum Post by Ryan Fleming
author: jennao
description: Presents a Platinum member argument that some core Synoptic Gospel material may predate Paul's letters.

title: Is Papias Generally Trustworthy?
author: BDEhrman
description: Evaluates Papias's reliability as evidence for traditional claims about the authorship of Matthew and Mark.

title: Does Papias Provide Us Direct Access to the Teachings of the Apostles?
author: BDEhrman
description: Examines whether Papias provides direct access to apostolic teaching or later traditions about Gospel authorship.

title: Papias in a Nutshell.  An Important Figure Among the Apostolic Fathers
author: BDEhrman
description: Introduces Papias as a controversial Apostolic Father whose fragments became important for debates about Gospel authorship.

title: Announcing the “Sponsor a Stranger” Campaign
author: BDEhrman
description: Announces the Sponsor a Stranger campaign connected with Love Thy Stranger and the blog's charitable work.

title: Free Live Debate on the Resurrection (March 2)
author: BDEhrman
description: Announces a free live debate on the historical evidence for Jesus' resurrection.

title: The Earliest Christian Apologist:  Quadratus in a Nutshell
author: BDEhrman
description: Introduces Quadratus as the earliest surviving Christian apologist and explains the significance of Christian apology.

title: What Are the Chances?  The Logic of “Intelligent Design”
author: BDEhrman
description: Considers the logic of intelligent design arguments about the origin of life from a nonexpert perspective.

title: Interesting Questions from Readers (Including on the Consistency of Mark and the Reality of Suffering)
author: BDEhrman
description: Responds to reader questions about Mark's consistency, scribal changes, suffering, and studying the New Testament without Greek.

title: Who Wrote the Shepherd of Hermas, and When?
author: BDEhrman
description: Explains what can be known about the authorship and date of the Shepherd of Hermas.

title: What’s Actually *in* the Shepherd of Hermas?
author: BDEhrman
description: Summarizes the contents of the Shepherd of Hermas, including its revelations, angelic messages, and moral instruction.

title: Blog Dinner in Charlottesville, VA on March 21.  You Interested?
author: BDEhrman
description: Announces a blog dinner in Charlottesville, Virginia, with attendance details for interested members.

title: A Sign From God?  Mormon Missionaries and Me
author: BDEhrman
description: Tells a story about Mormon missionaries and a supposed divine sign from the author's evangelical youth.

title: If It Wasn’t “For Your Sins” Why Did Jesus Die (according to Luke)?
author: BDEhrman
description: Explains Luke's view of Jesus' death when Luke does not present it as an atoning sacrifice for sins.

author: jennao
description: Announces the March 2026 Gold Q&A and invites Gold and Platinum members to submit questions.

author: BDEhrman
description: Examines whether Luke removed or softened the idea that Jesus' death was for sins.

author: BDEhrman
description: Explains the textual problem in Luke 22:19-20 and its importance for whether Luke portrays Jesus' death as for others.

title: Should You Say It Like You Mean It?  Those Inoffensive Critical Scholars…
author: BDEhrman
description: Reflects on why critical scholars often state controversial conclusions in muted or inoffensive ways.

title: Yet More: A Christian Forger Who Warns Against Reading Forgeries!
author: BDEhrman
description: Discusses the irony of a forged Christian text warning readers against forged writings.

title: And an Ancient Christian Deceived Deceiver (i.e. Forger)
author: BDEhrman
description: Examines an early Christian example of a forger warning against deception while using deception.

title: Should You “Give It All Away”?  The Views of Wealth in the Shepherd of Hermas
author: BDEhrman
description: Compares Jesus' radical teaching on wealth with the softer approach to charitable giving in the Shepherd of Hermas.

title: An Ancient Apocalypse Among the Apostolic Fathers: The Shepherd of Hermas
author: BDEhrman
description: Introduces the Shepherd of Hermas as an early Christian apocalypse among the Apostolic Fathers.

title: A Great Story of a Deceived Deceiver
author: BDEhrman
description: Recounts an ancient story about a forger deceived by another forgery, used to introduce literary deception.

title: The Second Coming (Yeats)
author: BDEhrman
description: Explains how Yeats's The Second Coming depends on Christian imagery and religious literacy.

title: About Work Habits and Productivity…
author: BDEhrman
description: Discusses work habits, productivity, and the author's earlier approach to getting large amounts of writing done.

title: Why I Wrote Love Thy Stranger and Significant Benefits that Can Come Your Way
author: BDEhrman
description: Explains why Love Thy Stranger was written and describes benefits connected with pre-ordering the book.

title: Greek Island Trip: Cancellation
author: BDEhrman
description: Announces the cancellation of a planned Greek Islands lecture cruise.

title: From Eternal Torment to Styles of Greek to the Dating of Ignatius: Interesting Readers’ Questions
author: BDEhrman
description: Responds to reader questions about eternal torment, Paul's gospel, Greek style, and the dating of Ignatius.

description: Presents a translated excerpt from the History of Joseph the Carpenter in which Jesus narrates Joseph's illness, life, and death.

author: BDEhrman
description: Introduces the History of Joseph the Carpenter as a little-known apocryphal Gospel that fills gaps in traditions about Joseph.

description: Explains why second-century Christians valued authoritative writings without yet having a closed New Testament canon.

author: BDEhrman
description: Summarizes the criteria church fathers used when deciding which writings belonged in the New Testament canon.

author: BDEhrman
description: Explains why early Christians, already using Jewish Scripture, came to want a distinct collection of authoritative Christian writings.

author: jennao
description: Shares the recording of a final UNC retirement lecture on a major discovery in biblical studies.

description: Shares a documentary and full interview related to Christ and the Gods.
